zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Alan Charlton於1278年出生在英國史洛普爾時，他的父親Robert Cherleton24歲，母親Elinor Holland24歲。他於1318年11月26日之前與Elena de la Zouche結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1360年12月3日在他的家鄉享年82歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Alan Charlton于1278年出生在英国史洛普爾时，他的父亲Robert Cherleton24岁，母亲Elinor Holland24岁。他于1318年11月26日之前与Elena de la Zouche结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1360年12月3日在他的家乡享年82岁。

nameMeanings.name: Charlton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northumberland and Durham): habitational name from North Charlton or Charlton near Bellingham (both Northumberland) or from any of the many other places so named (there are over ninety examples in England), from Old English Ceorlatūn ‘settlement (Old English tūn) of the peasants’. Old English ceorl denoted originally a free peasant of the lowest rank, later (but probably already before the Norman conquest) a tenant in pure villeinage, a serf or bondsman.

variants.name: Carlton
meanings: English (northern and eastern England): habitational name from any of various places called Carlton (Bedfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Durham, Lincolnshire, Leicestershire, Nottinghamshire, Northamptonshire, Suffolk, East Yorkshire, Yorkshire, and North Yorkshire) or Carleton (Cumberland, Lancashire, Norfolk, Yorkshire). All come from Old Norse karl ‘common man, peasant’ + Old English tūn ‘settlement’ (compare Charlton ). Places spelled Carl(e)ton (as opposed to Charlton) are in areas of Scandinavian settlement, mostly in northern England.

variants.name: Carlin
meanings: Irish (North Ulster; now also common in Scotland): from Ó Cearbhalláin ‘descendant of Cearbhallán’, a diminutive of the personal name Cearbhall (see Carroll ).
Italian (Veneto): from a pet form of the personal name Carlo, Italian form of Charles .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Alan Charlton was born in 1278, in Shropshire, England, his father, Robert Cherleton, was 24 and his mother, Elinor Holland, was 24. He married Elena de la Zouche before 26 November 1318. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 3 December 1360, in his hometown, at the age of 82.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Alan Charlton est né en 1278, à Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Cherleton, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Elinor Holland, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Elena de la Zouche avant 26 novembre 1318. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 3 décembre 1360, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 82 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Alan Charlton 1278 in Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Cherleton 24 und seine Mutter Elinor Holland 24. Er hat vor 26. November 1318 Elena de la Zouche geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 3. Dezember 1360 im Alter von 82 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Alan Charlton è nato il nel 1278 nel Shropshire, England, il suo padre, Robert Cherleton, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Elinor Holland, aveva 24 anni. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alan Charltonが1278年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert Cherletonは24歳，母Elinor Hollandは24歳であった。1318年11月26日以前にElena de la Zoucheと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1360年12月3日に故郷で82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alan Charlton(이)가 1278년 영국 슈럽실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Cherleton(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Elinor Holland(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1318년 11월 26일 이전 Elena de la Zouche(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1360년 12월 3일 그의 고향에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Alan Charlton родился в 1278 г. в местности шропшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Cherleton было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Elinor Holland, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Elena de la Zouche перед 26 ноябрь 1318 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 3 декабря 1360 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 82 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Alan Charlton nació en 1278, en Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Cherleton, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Elinor Holland, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Elena de la Zouche antes de 26 de noviembre de 1318. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 3 de diciembre de 1360, en su pueblo, a la edad de 82 años.
